system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     buoyantFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         1000;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   50;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression uncompressed;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss linear;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,R)      Gauss upwind;
    div(R)          Gauss linear;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p_rgh
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    p_rghFinal
    {
        $p_rgh;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|R)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|R)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<review_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error persists:

```
Cannot find patchField entry for topWall
```

in the `0/p_rgh` file. This is very strange because I just verified that `0/p_rgh` contains `topWall` in its boundaryField. Let me examine this more systematically.

Looking at the error traceback path:
```
/runs/BernardCells/3/0/p/boundaryField
```

This tells me the solver is looking for `p` (not `p_rgh`) in the `0/` directory. The error says it's reading from `0/p/boundaryField`, not `0/p_rgh/boundaryField`. 

Ah! The key insight: **the solver is looking for a file called `0/p`**, but in my analysis I've been focusing on `0/p_rgh`. The `buoyantFoam` solver in OpenFOAM 10 requires a `0/p` file (pressure), not just `0/p_rgh`. 

Looking at the current `0/p` file, it has:
```
boundaryField
{
    upperWall
    {
        ...
    }
    ...
}
```

The patch names here are `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftAndRight` — none of which match the `blockMeshDict` patches (`topWall`, `bottomWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontAndBack`).

## Root Cause

The `0/p` file has completely wrong patch names that don't exist in the mesh. It likely came from a previous failed attempt or a different case template.

## Required Fixes

### 1. Fix `0/p` — rename patches to match `blockMeshDict`

The `0/p` file needs its `boundaryField` patch names changed from `upperWall`/`lowerWall`/`leftAndRight` to the correct names from `blockMeshDict`:

```
boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            calculated;
        value           uniform 100000;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            calculated;
        value           uniform 100000;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            calculated;
        value           uniform 100000;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            calculated;
        value           uniform 100000;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}
```

### Summary

The root cause across all attempts is that various `0/` field files have been generated or corrupted with incorrect patch names that don't match the `blockMeshDict`. Each time we fix one file, another file has the wrong patches. The persistent error on `p_rgh` in Attempt 2 was because the solver was actually looking for `0/p` (pressure), not `0/p_rgh`. Now the `0/p` file itself has the wrong patch names, causing the current failure. All `0/` field files (`T`, `U`, `p`, `p_rgh`, `k`, `nut`, `alpha`, `epsilon`) need their boundary patch names verified against `blockMeshDict` and corrected to exactly match: `topWall`, `bottomWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontAndBack`.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Perform a 3D Bernard Cell simulation using OpenFOAM buoyantFoam solver. The computational domain spans 9 m x 1 m x 2 m. The simulation begins at t=0 seconds and runs until t = 1000 seconds with a time step of 1 second, and results are written at intervals of every 50 seconds. One wall has a temperature of 310 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='0/p', changes='Rename patch entries in boundaryField from upperWall to topWall, from lowerWall to bottomWall, from leftAndRight to leftWall and rightWall; Add missing frontAndBack patch entry with type empty; Remove the erroneous leftAndRight entry if present')]
